summary: This brief post uses a clever visual joke—bumper stickers and t-shirts showing 'I ♥ Unicode' rendered with HTML entities—to highlight the persistent challenges of character encoding in software development. Atwood poses the rhetorical question of why this is funny, implying that programmers who understand Unicode pain will immediately get the joke. He provides educational resources for those who want to understand the underlying issues. The post serves as both humor and a gentle nudge for developers to learn about Unicode properly.
key_points: The humor derives from the irony of Unicode-related merchandise failing to properly render Unicode characters
Understanding why this joke is funny requires knowledge of character encoding issues
Proper Unicode knowledge is essential for modern software developers
Atwood curates key resources including Joel Spolsky's famous Unicode article
money_quotes: Here's my rhetorical question to you: why is this funny?
Here's hoping the next programmer who sees this is laughing, too – and for the right reasons.
key_insight: If you don't immediately understand why 'I ♥ Unicode' merchandise is hilarious, you need to learn more about character encoding.
